threats.title: Incorrect Token1 Premium Sign Inverts NAV Impact
threats.hypothesis_description: The calculation for token1 pool exposure subtracts earned premiums (short) from owed premiums (long), which is the inverse of the correct logic used for token0. This causes earned token1 premiums to artificially decrease the vault's NAV and owed token1 premiums to artificially increase it.
threats.investigation_rationale: This fundamental accounting error directly manipulates the share price. An attacker can observe the vault's premium state and queue deposits when NAV is artificially low (getting shares at a discount) or queue withdrawals when NAV is artificially high (extracting excess assets).
preconditions: Vault has active positions with non-zero token1 premiums

initial_economic_evidence.suspicious_calculation: poolExposure1 = long - short (inverts standard short - long logic)
initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: NAV is systematically miscalculated based on token1 premium balance
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: Attacker deposits during artificial NAV dips and withdraws during artificial NAV spikes to extract value from the vault.

economic_questions: How much can the NAV be skewed by typical token1 premium balances?
Can an attacker predict manager fulfillments to guarantee profitable entry/exit?
validation_requirements: Verify Panoptic protocol's leftSlot/rightSlot premium accounting conventions
Calculate potential value extraction based on historical premium sizes

preliminary_attack_economics.estimated_capital_needed: Sufficient capital to make a meaningful deposit/withdrawal
preliminary_attack_economics.estimated_execution_cost: Standard gas costs for request and execute transactions
preliminary_attack_economics.potential_profit_range: Proportional to the size of the token1 premium miscalculation
preliminary_attack_economics.estimated_profit_margin: Variable, depends on vault's open positions
preliminary_attack_economics.attack_duration: Multi-epoch (request in epoch N, execute in N+1)

market_conditions_analysis.liquidity_requirements: Vault must have sufficient underlying token liquidity to pay out inflated withdrawals
market_conditions_analysis.optimal_timing: When vault has large unbalanced token1 premiums
market_conditions_analysis.market_impact_assessment: Directly drains value from existing shareholders

potential_victim_impact.total_value_at_risk: Significant portion of vault assets depending on premium size

investigation_priorities.parameter_analysis: Compare poolExposure0 and poolExposure1 logic against Panoptic docs
investigation_priorities.calculation_validation: Test NAV output with mock premium values

threats.title: Per-Pool Exposure Clamping Artificially Inflates NAV
threats.hypothesis_description: computeNAV clamps each pool's combined exposure to zero before summing them. If the vault has an underwater position (negative exposure) in one pool and a profitable position in another, the debt is ignored, artificially inflating the total NAV.
threats.investigation_rationale: This allows withdrawing users to extract more assets than their fair share based on an inflated share price, leaving the remaining users to bear the hidden debt when positions are eventually settled.
preconditions: At least one pool position is net negative (underwater)

initial_economic_evidence.suspicious_calculation: Math.max(poolExposure, 0) per pool instead of aggregate
initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: Debt in specific pools is erased from NAV calculation
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: Withdrawers receive payouts based on gross assets rather than net assets, stealing from remaining depositors.

economic_questions: How frequently do individual Panoptic pools go net negative?
What is the maximum NAV inflation possible under normal market conditions?
validation_requirements: Simulate multi-pool portfolio with mixed profitable/underwater positions

preliminary_attack_economics.potential_profit_range: Equal to the user's pro-rata share of the hidden debt
preliminary_attack_economics.estimated_profit_margin: Directly proportional to the size of the underwater position

market_conditions_analysis.liquidity_requirements: Vault must have sufficient free assets to fulfill the inflated withdrawal
market_conditions_analysis.optimal_timing: During market volatility when one pool takes a loss
market_conditions_analysis.market_impact_assessment: Socializes losses to remaining users while privatizing gains

potential_victim_impact.estimated_impact_per_user: Proportional absorption of the hidden debt
potential_victim_impact.total_value_at_risk: 100% of the underwater position's negative value

investigation_priorities.calculation_validation: Compare per-pool clamping vs aggregate clamping

threats.title: Free Token Balance Can Be Erased by Pool Debt
threats.hypothesis_description: The vault's free balance is added to poolExposure0/1 for the first pool that uses that token. If that specific pool has massive debt, the debt offsets the free balance before the per-pool clamping to zero. This effectively erases the free balance from the total NAV.
threats.investigation_rationale: This understates the NAV, allowing new depositors to acquire shares at an artificially low price. Furthermore, it can cause the totalAssets calculation in HypoVault to underflow if the erased free balance was backing reservedWithdrawalAssets, permanently bricking the vault.
preconditions: Vault holds free balance of a token
The first pool evaluated for that token has a net negative exposure exceeding the free balance

initial_economic_evidence.suspicious_calculation: Free balance is absorbed by pool debt before clamping
initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: NAV is artificially deflated by the exact amount of the free balance
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: Depositors get shares at a steep discount, or the vault bricks due to underflow.

economic_questions: Can an attacker intentionally push a pool into debt to trigger this?
Does this reliably cause the totalAssets underflow in HypoVault?
validation_requirements: Test computeNAV with a large free balance and a larger pool debt

preliminary_attack_economics.estimated_capital_needed: Capital to deposit during the NAV deflation
preliminary_attack_economics.potential_profit_range: Up to the entire erased free balance value distributed to new shares
preliminary_attack_economics.estimated_profit_margin: Extremely high if free balance is large

market_conditions_analysis.optimal_timing: When a pool position goes heavily underwater
market_conditions_analysis.market_impact_assessment: Severe dilution of existing users or complete protocol halt

potential_victim_impact.estimated_impact_per_user: Severe dilution or permanent lock of funds

investigation_priorities.parameter_analysis: Analyze free balance assignment logic in computeNAV

threats.title: Vault Bricking via Total Assets Underflow During Drawdowns
threats.hypothesis_description: In HypoVault, totalAssets is calculated as NAV + 1 - assetsDeposited - reservedWithdrawalAssets. If the vault's NAV drops below the sum of pending deposits and reserved withdrawals (e.g., due to trading losses), this calculation underflows and reverts.
threats.investigation_rationale: Once the vault is underwater relative to its pending obligations, the manager can no longer fulfill ANY deposits or withdrawals. The vault becomes permanently bricked until the NAV recovers, trapping all pending user funds.

initial_economic_evidence.suspicious_calculation: Unsafe subtraction of pending liabilities from current NAV
initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: Normal market drawdowns trigger arithmetic underflow
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: No direct profit, but causes catastrophic denial of service and fund locking.

economic_questions: What happens to pending deposits if the vault never recovers?
Can the manager rescue funds using cancelDeposit/cancelWithdrawal?
validation_requirements: Simulate a trading loss that drops NAV below pending obligations
Attempt to call fulfillDeposits and confirm revert

market_conditions_analysis.optimal_timing: During market downturns affecting vault positions

potential_victim_impact.direct_victims: Users with pending deposits or withdrawals
potential_victim_impact.total_value_at_risk: All pending deposits and unexecuted withdrawals

threats.title: Permanent Loss of Usable Capital via Withdrawal Rounding Dust
threats.hypothesis_description: In executeWithdrawal, sharesToFulfill and assetsToWithdraw are calculated using integer division, which rounds down. The fractional dust assets remain in reservedWithdrawalAssets forever and can never be claimed.
threats.investigation_rationale: Because reservedWithdrawalAssets is subtracted from the vault's NAV during fulfillments, this accumulating dust permanently reduces the vault's effective capital and lowers the share price for all remaining users over time.
preconditions: Multiple users executing partial withdrawals over many epochs

initial_economic_evidence.suspicious_calculation: Double rounding down in pro-rata calculations leaves orphaned assets
initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: Dust accumulates in reservedWithdrawalAssets, permanently reducing totalAssets
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: Systematic value bleed from the active vault capital into an unclaimable reserve.

economic_questions: How fast does this dust accumulate under high transaction volume?
Does the accumulated dust materially impact the share price?
validation_requirements: Measure the growth of orphaned reservedWithdrawalAssets

market_conditions_analysis.market_impact_assessment: Slow, permanent drag on vault performance

threats.title: Permanent Lock of User Funds via Zero-Amount Epoch Fulfillment
threats.hypothesis_description: If the manager fulfills an epoch with assetsToFulfill = 0 or sharesToFulfill = 0, the epoch state records 0 for the fulfilled amount. Subsequent calls by users to executeDeposit or executeWithdrawal for that epoch will revert due to division by zero.
threats.investigation_rationale: Since the epoch has already advanced, the manager cannot use cancelDeposit or cancelWithdrawal to rescue the funds. The users' queued assets or shares are permanently locked in the contract with no recovery path.
preconditions: Manager calls fulfillDeposits or fulfillWithdrawals with a 0 amount
Users have queued requests in that epoch

initial_economic_evidence.suspicious_calculation: Division by assetsFulfilled/sharesFulfilled without non-zero checks
initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: Zero fulfillment creates a poison pill epoch that reverts all executions
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: No direct profit, but allows a compromised or buggy manager to permanently destroy user funds.

economic_questions: Is there any edge case where a manager might legitimately fulfill 0?
Can a user bypass the division by zero if their queued amount is also 0?
validation_requirements: Verify that cancelDeposit cannot rescue funds from past epochs

market_conditions_analysis.optimal_timing: Any time users have queued funds
market_conditions_analysis.market_impact_assessment: Complete loss of funds for affected users

potential_victim_impact.direct_victims: Users who queued requests in the poisoned epoch
potential_victim_impact.total_value_at_risk: All funds queued in the specific epoch

investigation_priorities.calculation_validation: Confirm division by zero revert propagates

threats.title: Fee-on-Transfer Tokens Cause NAV Deflation and Share Dilution
threats.hypothesis_description: requestDeposit records the exact assets amount requested, but if the underlying token has a transfer fee, the vault receives less. The recorded assetsDeposited is subtracted from the NAV during fulfillment, which artificially deflates totalAssets and causes the vault to issue too many shares to the depositor.
threats.investigation_rationale: This allows an attacker to systematically drain value from the vault by repeatedly depositing and withdrawing a fee-on-transfer token, as the inflated share issuance outweighs the transfer fees, diluting all other users.
deployment_assumptions: The underlyingToken is a fee-on-transfer token
threats.config_evidence: No deployment script provided to verify token semantics, but code assumes standard ERC20.

proof_of_signal.pattern: queuedDeposit[msg.sender][currentEpoch] += assets; without checking actual received balance

preconditions: underlyingToken implements a fee on transfer
Vault has existing TVL to dilute

initial_economic_evidence.suspicious_calculation: totalAssets = NAV + 1 - assetsDeposited (where assetsDeposited > actual received)
initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: totalAssets is artificially lowered, increasing sharesReceived per asset
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: Attacker gains disproportionate share of the vault, allowing them to withdraw more underlying tokens than they deposited (net of fees).

economic_questions: Does the share inflation exceed the round-trip transfer fees?
How quickly can an attacker drain the vault?
validation_requirements: Deploy with a mock fee-on-transfer token
Calculate exact share issuance vs actual token balance

preliminary_attack_economics.estimated_capital_needed: Moderate capital to cycle through deposits/withdrawals
preliminary_attack_economics.potential_profit_range: Depends on the fee percentage and vault TVL
preliminary_attack_economics.estimated_profit_margin: Profitable if share inflation > 2x transfer fee

market_conditions_analysis.liquidity_requirements: Vault must have existing liquidity to absorb the dilution

investigation_priorities.parameter_analysis: Analyze totalAssets calculation with mismatched balances
investigation_priorities.calculation_validation: Test exploit profitability with 1% and 5% fee tokens

threats.title: NAV Manipulation via TWAP Oracle Exploitation
threats.hypothesis_description: computeNAV validates manager-provided prices against a TWAP oracle. If the manager is an automated bot that sources prices from the same oracle or adjusts its prices to stay within maxPriceDeviation of the TWAP, an attacker can manipulate the underlying Uniswap pool to skew the TWAP.
threats.investigation_rationale: By manipulating the TWAP, the attacker forces the manager to submit manipulated prices (or creates a DoS if the manager submits honest prices that revert). This alters the vault's NAV, allowing the attacker to extract value during deposit or withdrawal fulfillments.
preconditions: twapWindow is short enough to be manipulated economically
Manager bot logic relies on or follows the TWAP

initial_economic_evidence.suspicious_calculation: Reliance on TWAP for price bounds without secondary oracle validation
initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: Attacker skews Uniswap pool price over the TWAP window to shift the acceptable price band
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: Attacker queues deposit/withdrawal, manipulates TWAP, manager fulfills at manipulated NAV, attacker extracts profit.

economic_questions: What is the configured twapWindow length?
How much capital is required to move the TWAP by maxPriceDeviation?
validation_requirements: Analyze cost of TWAP manipulation vs potential extraction from vault

preliminary_attack_economics.estimated_capital_needed: Large capital to sustain Uniswap pool imbalance over TWAP window
preliminary_attack_economics.estimated_execution_cost: Swap fees and impermanent loss during manipulation
preliminary_attack_economics.potential_profit_range: Up to maxPriceDeviation % of the vault's TVL
preliminary_attack_economics.estimated_profit_margin: Profitable if vault TVL is significantly larger than pool liquidity

market_conditions_analysis.liquidity_requirements: Low liquidity in the Uniswap pool relative to vault TVL
market_conditions_analysis.optimal_timing: Periods of low organic trading volume
market_conditions_analysis.market_impact_assessment: Direct extraction of value from vault

potential_victim_impact.total_value_at_risk: Percentage of TVL equal to maxPriceDeviation

investigation_priorities.calculation_validation: Calculate manipulation cost vs vault extraction

threats.title: Profitable Withdrawals Blocked if Fee Wallet is Unset
threats.hypothesis_description: If the owner does not set a feeWallet (leaving it as address(0)) and performanceFeeBps > 0, any profitable withdrawal will attempt to transfer the fee to address(0). For standard ERC20 tokens that revert on transfers to the zero address, this will cause executeWithdrawal to revert.
threats.investigation_rationale: Users with profitable positions will be unable to execute their withdrawals and claim their assets until the owner intervenes, effectively locking their funds.
deployment_assumptions: underlyingToken reverts on transfer to address(0)
threats.config_evidence: feeWallet is not set in the constructor, requiring a separate setFeeWallet call.

initial_economic_evidence.suspicious_calculation: Missing zero-address check before fee transfer
initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: Uninitialized state variable causes core functionality to revert
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: No direct profit, but causes denial of service for successful users.

economic_questions: Can users bypass the fee by transferring shares to reset basis?
Is there a way to force the withdrawal to be unprofitable to bypass the revert?
validation_requirements: Test executeWithdrawal with address(0) fee wallet and standard ERC20
Verify if basis manipulation can bypass the fee logic
